description_long: This is a bulbous, double-handled ceramic vase with a wide, flared mouth and a narrow neck. The body is primarily a matte terracotta color, with a large vertical band of white slip on the front, adorned with intricate geometric and symbolic motifs, including a sun-like emblem and triangular patterns. The handles are small and rounded, positioned symmetrically on either side of the body. The surface shows a combination of painted and incised decorations, with some areas featuring cross-hatched textures. The back of the vase is mostly unadorned, displaying the natural reddish-brown clay. The overall appearance suggests a handcrafted, possibly ancient or ethnographic style, suitable for decorative display in a public or semi-public urban setting.
description: A bulbous terracotta vase with two small handles, a flared mouth, and intricate geometric and symbolic motifs on a white slip front panel. The back is plain reddish-brown clay, highlighting its handcrafted, decorative nature.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white slip panel with geometric motifs, sun emblem, and both handles visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the transition from decorated white slip to plain terracotta, with one handle and cross-hatched texture visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain reddish-brown clay surface and both handles, with no decorative motifs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other handle, the edge of the decorated panel, and the cross-hatched texture.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
